entries.content:
- Auto-generate event teasers for all 5 accounts (different angles per persona)
- Schedule posts around event lifecycle: announce → hype → day-of → recap
- AI content generation for BASSLINE news posts and SYNTH.AI track announcements
- WhatsApp broadcast for Alex Rivera's exclusive lists

- NOCTVRNAL films the events → Mia Noir is IN the content → Alex Rivera promoted the event → SYNTH.AI played the set → BASSLINE reports on it
- All 5 accounts mention each other naturally
- All ticket links go through Kartis with tracked promo codes
- TBM automates cross-posting across all accounts
- Content calendar synced to Kartis event schedule

- TBP owns audience growth, event brands, promoters, content
- Kartis owns events, ticket sales, table mgmt, promoter attribution, check-in
- Future Productions/FTRL delivers DJ profiles and media
- Remote Island is the design quality benchmark
- TBM is the marketing engine (starting with WhatsApp, expanding to FB groups, SMS/RCS, email, content gen, social mgr)
- TB Group is the parent umbrella above all properties

- **Type:** AI music + AI visuals content factory
- **Angle:** Trippy audio-reactive visualizations synced to house, techno, EDM, and psytrance. AI-generated music (Suno) meets AI-generated visuals (Deforum, TouchDesigner, RunwayML). Occasionally features real songs added by the team.
- **Platforms:** Instagram Reels, TikTok (primary), YouTube (long-form visual trips)
- **Content:**
  - 15-60s psychedelic visualizations synced to drops/beats
  - "Trip Report" series — full track visual journeys
8 departments: Growth, Events, Product/Kartis, Marketing Automation, Partnerships, Promoter Ops, Revenue Ops, Short-Form Content

- Kartis + FTRL share Prisma schema (PostgreSQL)
- Kartis: 30+ API routes, Stripe, QR, Pusher, Nodemailer, Sentry, i18n (EN/HE)
- WBpro: Render (persistent disk for WhatsApp session)
- whatsapp-fly: 99% duplicate of WBpro (can archive)
- Israeli Day: Next.js 16 (only non-Astro site)
- FB Group Poster: BullMQ + Redis + Puppeteer (Docker)

- **Type:** Industry news/curator account
- **Angle:** The CNN of house, techno, and EDM. First to report, best analysis, hottest takes.
- **Platforms:** Instagram, TikTok, X/Twitter, YouTube
- **Content:**
  - Breaking news graphics (new releases, festival lineups, DJ drama)
  - "Track of the Day" — 30s clip + review
  - Weekly "Top 10" countdown videos
  - Interview clips with DJs (even if fan-made compilation)
  - Festival coverage recaps
  - "This Day in Dance Music History"
  - Meme content

- Month 1 (Core Setup): TBP fixes, Kartis event MVP, checkout+QR, short-form content ramp
- Month 2-3 (Growth Flywheel): Promoter links+commissions, marketing OS rollout
- Month 4-6 (Scale & Polish): Table maps, multi-currency, full automation

- Use **subagents** for quick parallel tasks (what we do now)
- Use **agent teams** for complex multi-step coordination
- Use **batch API** for bulk non-urgent processing (e.g., reviewing 100 files)

- Run Kartis seed scripts: `cd ~/ecosystem/kartis-astro/src && npx tsx prisma/seed-events.ts && npx tsx prisma/seed-promoters.ts && npx tsx prisma/seed-cms.ts`
- Set KARTIS_WEBHOOK_SECRET on TBM + WBpro Vercel/Render env vars
- Test Stripe checkout end-to-end
- Test promoter tracking: tbp-website-astro.vercel.app/p/DANNY25

- **Type:** Real-looking persona (AI-generated or model)
- **Angle:** NYC party girl who's always at the best events. Aspirational lifestyle. "She's everywhere."
- **Platforms:** Instagram (primary), TikTok, YouTube Shorts
- **Content framework (high-engagement formula):**
  - GRWM (Get Ready With Me) for events — outfit reveal, makeup, pregame
  - "Come party with me" vlogs — first-person event coverage
  - "Rating NYC clubs" series — honest reviews with scores
  - "Things you NEED to know befo

- NYC: Israeli Thursdays, Rooftop Revelry, After Hours Xclusive, Birthday Bash, RSVP Recharge
- MIA: Poolside Fiesta, River Nights, Elegant Chaos, MIA Pass, Underground Beats

1. **Subagents** (Agent tool) — quick, focused, report back. What we've been using.
2. **Agent Teams** — full sessions that talk to each other. New experimental.
3. **Git Worktrees** — manual parallel sessions on different branches.
4. **Batch API via MCP** — 50% cheaper for non-urgent work.

Auth, clients, accounts (with Puppeteer login flow), groups (with bulk import), campaigns (with launch/pause/resume/cancel), posts, templates, content (AI variations + media upload), dashboard stats, settings

```bash
cd ~/ecosystem/kartis-astro/src && npx vercel --prod
cd ~/ecosystem/tbm/src && npx vercel --prod
cd ~/ecosystem/ftrl-dj/src && npx vercel --prod
cd ~/ecosystem/remote-island/src && npx vercel --prod
cd ~/ecosystem/future-productions/src && npx vercel --prod
cd ~/ecosystem/tbp-website-astro/src && npx vercel --prod
cd ~/ecosystem/israeli-day && npx vercel --prod
```
entries.summary: Deploy Queue (Vercel rate limit hit — redeploy when resets)

entries.content:
- **Type:** Faceless aesthetic/mood account
- **Angle:** The visual identity of nightlife. No face, no voice — just pure aesthetic. Dark, cinematic, aspirational. Think Berghain meets NYC rooftops.
- **Platforms:** Instagram (grid aesthetic), TikTok (mood edits), Pinterest (mood boards)
- **Content:** Cinematic night city edits (shot on iPhone, graded dark), DJ booth POVs with no talking, crowd energy slow-mo, "the vibe" compilations, aesthetic quotes overlaid on nightlife footage, venue reveals

- Tickets: 20% / 30% (tiered by performance)
- Tables: 7% / 12% (tiered by performance)

- **Enable**: `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` in settings.json
- Already enabled in this session
- **How it works**: One lead session + multiple teammate sessions
- Teammates have their own context windows, communicate via shared task list and mailbox
- **Best for**: 3-5 teammates, 5-6 tasks per teammate
- **Commands**: Tell Claude to "create an agent team" in natural language
- **Display modes**: in-process (default) or split panes (tmux/iTerm2)
- Uses **~7x tokens** of a single sessio

- Prisma v7 requires PrismaPg driver adapter (see server/lib/prisma.ts)
- Post worker: concurrency 3, account rotation, circuit breaker (50% failure auto-pause)
- Maintenance worker: daily counter reset, session health checks, recurring campaign checks
- Anti-detection: randomized delays, human-like typing, fingerprint randomization, proxy support
- Multi-account support with round-robin rotation and cooldowns
- 3 content modes: manual, templates with {{variables}}, AI-generated variations
- 3 s

### Revenue Layer — DONE
- All ticket CTAs → Kartis (TBP, Israeli Day, Remote Island)
- Zero external ticketing links
- Promoter /p/[code] tracking redirect
- Customer-facing table picker (Konva, mobile pinch-zoom)
- QR code images in ticket confirmation emails
- CSV order export with promoter attribution
- Analytics dashboard (revenue, orders, promoters, check-ins)
- Multi-currency (USD/ILS) verified + Tel Aviv seed event
- Waitlist system for sold-out tickets
- Refund management with Stripe AP
entries.summary: Master Plan Progress (2026-03-25 session — 38 agents, 8 waves)

entries.content:
- **Type:** Experimental/documentary
- **Angle:** The world's first fully transparent AI music producer. Every track made with Suno/Udio. Documents the entire process — no secrets.
- **Platforms:** TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, SoundCloud, Spotify
- **Content:** "Watch me make a track in 60 seconds" process videos, AI vs human blind tests, genre experiments (make reggaeton with AI, make drill with AI), reaction to own AI music, studio vlogs, release day content
- **Growth strategy:**

1. **Run seed** — `vercel env pull --environment production` then `npx tsx prisma/seed-real-events.ts`
2. **Verify Stripe checkout** end-to-end with seeded event
3. **Test QR check-in** with StaffCheckin page
4. **Build promoter onboarding form** on TBPL /apply
5. **Wire Israeli Day pages** to use lib/kartis.ts (need API route for client components)
6. **Build Kartis webhook system** for TBM auto-campaigns
7. **Connect TBP subscribe** to Resend email

- **Repo**: https://github.com/s2-streamstore/claude-batch-toolkit
- Install: `git clone ... && ./install.sh --api-key sk-ant-...`
- Adds `send_to_batch` tool to Claude Code
- Send non-urgent work at 50% cost directly from this CLI
- Supports Anthropic API and Google Vertex backends

- Node.js 20 + Express + TypeScript
- React 18 + Vite + Tailwind CSS (dark mode)
- PostgreSQL (Prisma v7 with driver adapter) + Redis (BullMQ)
- Puppeteer (browser automation, anti-detection)
- AI Gateway (post variation generation)
- S3-compatible storage (media uploads)
- Docker + Render deployment

| Property | URL | Status |
|----------|-----|--------|
| TBP | tbp-website-astro.vercel.app | Live — all CTAs→Kartis, SEO/OG, promoter tracking |
| Kartis | kartis-astro.vercel.app/en | Live — full ticketing OS with 30+ APIs |
| TB Group | tb-group.vercel.app | Live (100%) |
| TBPL | tbpl.vercel.app | Live — dashboard, auth, apply form |
| TBM | tbm.vercel.app | Live — 6 modules complete |
| Israeli Day | israeli-day.vercel.app | Live — Kartis CTAs, birthdays, gallery |
| FTRL | ftrl-dj.vercel.

### Audit Phase (4 parallel agents)
- TBP, Kartis, Marketing stack, Talent/Brand all audited simultaneously
- Kartis was 95% built (major surprise) — Stripe, QR, auth, commissions all working
- WBpro already had Kartis events integration
- TBM was empty scaffold
- whatsapp-fly = duplicate of WBpro (kill it)
- whatsapp-broadcast = dead fork (kill it)

### Build Phase (8 parallel agents)
All completed successfully:
1. TBP: AdminClient fix, Vercel Analytics, .env.example
2. Kartis: promoter attribu

- **Angle:** NYC nightlife insider. Behind-the-scenes at the best parties. "I get you in."
- **Platforms:** Instagram, TikTok, WhatsApp broadcast
- **Content:** Event teasers (15s hype clips), crowd POV videos, VIP table tours, "what to wear" guides, ticket giveaways, day-in-the-life of a promoter
- **Growth strategy:** Collab with venue accounts, tag artists/DJs, location-based hashtags, WhatsApp broadcast lists for exclusive early access
- **Monetization:** Pr

- ✅ TBP /admin fixed — always redirects to Kartis CMS
- ✅ TB Group, TBPL, TBM — scaffolded, deployed to Vercel, pushed to GitHub
- ✅ Kartis promoter public site API (`/api/promoter/public-site?slug=xxx`)
- ✅ Kartis click-tracking redirect (`/p/[code]` → event page with ref)
- ✅ Israeli Day Kartis integration layer (`lib/kartis.ts`)
- ✅ Real event seeder (`prisma/seed-real-events.ts`) — reads TBP events into Kartis

- **URL**: `POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages/batches`
- **50% cheaper** than standard API calls
- Up to **10,000 requests per batch**
- Processed within **24 hours** (usually 30min-1hr)
- Results available for 29 days
- Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`

### Usage:
```bash
curl https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages/batches \
  -H "x-api-key: $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" \
  -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"requests":[{"custom_id":"req-1","params":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-20250514","max

name: agent_autonomy_expectations
description: Agents should be domain experts who proactively know what to build. User shouldn't have to bring tasks — the AI agency runs the real company.
type: feedback
---

Agents are experts running TB Group as an AI agency operating a real nightlife company. They should KNOW what needs to be done without being told every detail.

**Why:** The user runs a real business. The agent swarm IS the company's engineering/marketing/ops team. They should think like ni

name: batching_swarming
description: User wants agents batched and swarmed — spawn max parallel agents in single messages, never sequential when possible
type: feedback
---

Always batch agent spawns — launch ALL independent agents in ONE message with multiple tool calls.
Always swarm — when a task can be split into independent pieces, split it across as many parallel agents as possible.

**Why:** The user builds fast and expects maximum parallelization. Sequential agent calls waste time. The wh

**How to apply:** When running or testing any project, use `localhost` / `dev`

entries.summary: - [project_aionui.md](project_aionui.md) - AionUi desktop AI cowork app setup, build, and installation details

entries.content:
name: cloud-only-during-build
description: Use only cloud/API models during active development, not local Ollama models
type: feedback
---

Use only cloud-based models (not local Ollama) while actively building the project.

**Why:** Local models like gemma3:27b consume ~22 GB RAM, which competes with development tools and builds.

**How to apply:** Don't suggest or start local Ollama models during development sessions. If Ollama is consuming resources, offer to unload immediately.

name: AionUi project setup
description: AionUi desktop AI cowork app - cloned, built, and installed on this Mac. Electron+TypeScript+React app with multi-agent support.
